# Frontend/Backend Assignment \- Document Intelligence Platform

To grab the internship, you are required to complete a small assignment. Early submissions are given preference.

In this task, you are required to create a full-stack web application with AI/RAG integration.

## Objective:

Build a **full-stack web application with AI integration** that processes book data and enables intelligent querying.

The system should:

* Collect book data using automation  
* Store and manage it in a backend  
* Generate AI-based insights  
* Support question-answering over books  
* Expose functionality via REST APIs  
* Provide a simple frontend interface

## Backend (Django REST Framework)

### GET APIs:

\- Lists all uploaded books from the database 

\- Retrieves all detail about each book

\- Recommends related books

### POST APIs:

\- Uploading and processing books and details 

\- Asking questions about books (RAG query endpoint) 

## Document Processing Engine

Create a python application that scrapes books and provides AI insights.

**Extra marks for handling caching, smart chunking strategies, and optimized embedding generation.**

1. Uses automation to **collect book data from the web (any site)**  
2. Stores it in a backend  
3. Uses AI to **generate insights about books**  
4. Exposes everything via APIs  
5. Displays results on a simple frontend

### AI Insight Generation

You must generate **at least 2 of the following**:

* **Summary:** Generate a short summary of the book  
* **Genre Classification:** Predict genre based on description  
* **Recommendation Logic:** “If you like X, you’ll like Y”  
* **Sentiment Analysis:** Analyze tone of reviews/description

### RAG Pipeline Implementation:

The system should implement a complete RAG pipeline that: 

\- Generates embeddings for user questions 

\- Performs similarity search across book chunks 

\- Constructs relevant context from retrieved chunks 

\- Generates contextual answers using LLM with source citations

## Frontend (ReactJS / NextJS with Tailwind CSS)

### User Interface:

The frontend should be developed using ReactJS or NextJS, styled with Tailwind CSS.

### Required Pages:

1. **Dashboard/Book Listing Page**:

   * List all uploaded books with title, author, rating / reviews, description (if available), book URL

     

2. **Q\&A Interface**:

   * Question input 

   * Answer display (Display summary / genre / recommendations)

     ![][image1]

3. **Book detail page**

   * Displays details about the book selected by the user

## Database Schema Hints:

Design your database with the following table structure (there can be more tables):

**Books table**: Store document metadata including title, author, rating / reviews, description (if available), book URL

## Tech Stack Requirements:

* **Backend**: Django REST Framework, Python

* **Database**: MySQL for metadata, ChromaDB/FAISS for vectors

* **Frontend**: ReactJS/NextJS with Tailwind CSS

* **AI Integration**: OpenAI API, Claude API, or **LM Studio** (recommended if 

* **Automation**: Selenium


### AI Integration Options:

**Option 1**: Use external APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic Claude) 

**Option 2**: Use LM Studio for local LLM hosting (recommended alternative)

**LM Studio** allows you to run language models locally without requiring external API keys. Download and set up LM Studio with models like Llama, Mistral, or Code Llama for document question-answering tasks. This provides a cost-effective solution and ensures data privacy.

## Deadline

**18 April, 2026, Saturday, 11:55 PM**

## Submission Format

* Create a GitHub repository and add your code to it.

* In the README, add:

  * Screenshots of the UI you have created

  * Setup instructions for running the application

  * API documentation

  * Sample questions and answers from your system

* Include a requirements.txt file with all dependencies

* Add samples for testing

* Fill your repo link in this form: [https://forms.gle/Fby8pMSmBJqjuVf56](https://forms.gle/Fby8pMSmBJqjuVf56). 

* Make sure the code is neat and readable with proper comments.

* Make sure the README.md has 3-4 screenshots of the UI

## Evaluation Criteria:

* **Functionality** (40%): Working RAG pipeline, accurate answers, proper citations

* **Code Quality** (25%): Clean, readable, well-structured code

* **UI/UX** (20%): User-friendly interface, responsive design

* **Innovation** (15%): Creative features, optimization techniques, error handling

## Bonus Points (Optional):

* Caching AI responses (avoid repeated calls)  
* Embedding-based similarity (vector search)  
* Async processing (Celery / background jobs)  
* Bulk scraping pipeline  
* Multi-page scraping  
* Loading states \+ UX polish

* Advanced chunking strategies (semantic chunking, overlapping windows)

* Saving chat history

For any doubts, feel free to drop a mail at [devgods99@gmail.com](mailto:devgods99@gmail.com) We’ll try to respond as soon as possible\!

In case you’re not able to complete it within the deadline, do submit the code even if a part of it doesn’t work. The effort and skills you demonstrate through your code matter.

**Note: An early submission will give you an advantage over others.**

## Helpful Resources:

* LM Studio: [https://lmstudio.ai/](https://lmstudio.ai/)  (for local LLM hosting)

* ChromaDB Documentation: [https://docs.trychroma.com/](https://docs.trychroma.com/) 

* Sentence Transformers: [https://www.sbert.net/](https://www.sbert.net/) 

* Django REST Framework: [https://www.django-rest-framework.org/](https://www.django-rest-framework.org/) 

* React File Upload: [https://react-dropzone.js.org/](https://react-dropzone.js.org/)
